column2: indūtus est Dominus fortitūdinem, et praecinxit sē. Etenim firmāvit orbem terrae quī nōn commovēbitur. ^ The Lord hath reigned, he is clothed with beauty
column3: the Lord is clothed with strength, and hath girded himself. For he hath established the world which shall not be moved.

column2: ā saeculō tū es. ^ Thy throne is prepared from of old

column1: 3 ^ Ēlevāvērunt flūmina, Domine, ēlevāvērunt flūmina vōcem suam~
column2: ēlevāvērunt flūmina fluctūs suōs, ^ The floods have lifted up, O Lord, the floods have lifted up their voice
column3: the floods have lifted up their waves,

column1: 4 ^ ā vōcibus aquārum multārum. Mīrābilēs ēlātiōnēs maris~
column2: mīrābilis in altīs Dominus. ^ with the noise of many waters. Wonderful are the exaltations of the sea
column3: wonderful is the Lord on high.

column1: 5 ^ Testimōnia tua crēdibilia facta sunt nimis~
column2: dōmum tuam decet sanctitūdō, Domine, in longitūdinem diērum. ^ Thy testimonies are become exceedingly credible
column3: holiness becometh thy house, O Lord, unto length of days.
